plainEnglish: Any feature that uses dragging—reordering a list, a slider, drag-and-drop—must also be operable with a single pointer without dragging, for example tap-to-select then tap-destination, or up/down buttons. Dragging is hard for many people with motor disabilities.
whyItMatters: Users with motor disabilities, tremors, or who use a head pointer often can't perform a sustained drag and need a no-drag alternative.
quickCheck: For every drag interaction (reorder, slider, drag-drop), confirm there's a no-drag alternative such as click-to-move buttons or tap targets.
commonMistakes: Drag-and-drop reordering with no up/down buttons or other alternative.
Sliders that can only be set by dragging the thumb, with no clickable track or text input.
Kanban-style boards where cards can only be moved by dragging.

codeExamples.label: Reorder via buttons, not just dragging
